title: Error: error creating container storage: could not find enough available IDs
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Can not create mulitple containers when msyql:8.0.29  is one of them, running as rootless.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman rm -a
    podman create --entrypoint /bin/bash -it --name=mysql mysql:8.0.29 (returns ok)
    podman create --entrypoint /bin/sh -it --name=alpine alpine:latest (returns error)
    **Error: error creating container storage: could not find enough available IDs**

3. podman rm -a
    podman create --entrypoint /bin/sh -it --name=alpine alpine:latest (returns ok)
    podman create --entrypoint /bin/bash -it --name=mysql mysql:8.0.29 (returns error)
    **Error: error creating container storage: could not find enough available IDs**

**Describe the results you received:**
Error: error creating container storage: could not find enough available IDs

**Describe the results you expected:**
Successful creation of mulitple containers when one of them is mysql:8.0.29 which works fine using `docker create`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
```
podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
         0       1000          1
         1     100000      65536
```
```
cat /etc/sub[ug]id
kris:100000:65536
kris:100000:65536
```
It looks like somthing is wrong with image mysql:8.0.29, but I can't find anything peculiar with it expect that it wants to mount volume:
/var/lib/mysql as user mysql:mysql (999:999), ouput from msyql-container:
```
root@88c4d55609ee:/# tail -2 /etc/passwd
_apt:x:100:65534::/nonexistent:/usr/sbin/nologin
mysql:x:999:999::/home/mysql:/bin/sh
```
It is always the last stage, `DEBU[0000] overlay: mount_data=`,  in the creation process that fails when using `--log-level=debug` no matter in which order containers are created. Other containers which have mounting requirement works fine e.g.
```
podman rm -a
podman create --entrypoint /bin/bash -it --name=nexus sonatype/nexus3:latest
podman create --entrypoint /bin/sh -it --name=alpine alpine:latest
```
or
```
podman rm -a
podman create --entrypoint /bin/sh -it --name=alpine alpine:latest
podman create --entrypoint /bin/bash -it --name=nexus sonatype/nexus3:latest
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

comments.body: Can you create a third container say on ubi8 after the mysql one fails?  Any chance you have been playing with user namespace --userns=auto?
Had `export PODMAN_USERNS="auto"` in bash-startup, removing it solve the problem.
